rulings.Case Title: CONS. OF RUTH LACHAPELLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/3/26 for the CIU report to be completed. (No earlier availability.) Issues that must be addressed before the continued hearing: (1) Please file verification (either a letter or final statement) of the NY Life Insurance policy that was canceled and netted the estate just $5,083.03. (2) The assets on hand include a deposit from a reported BoA account xx3046 that was never reported, either in an I&A or in the last accounting. (3) Please provide more information about the following disbursements: $151.70 to Black Angus on 12/16/25; $127.99 to Coach on 12/1/25; $720.00 reimbursement to one of the co-conservators for auto rental on 10/20/25; and $78.83 to Ulta on 11/24/25. (4) Reimbursements to any individual must indicate who the original payee was. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 7/20/26. (Proof of payment of the assessment fee, if ordered, will also need to be filed before the accounting can be approved.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The payment to Amy Harrington was apparently for the appraisal; the order appointing her as the probate referee did not order any specific amount of fee or commission. The FMV of assets shows in the accounting differs slightly from the amount stated in paragraph 6 of the petition. Subparagraph D of paragraph 9 of the petition is incomplete.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HOWARD PARKER JOHNSTON, JR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Granted per written order. The Court waives defective notice for this hearing only. Notice did not have the instructions for how to appear remotely attached to the notice; a printout of pages of the local rules that do not even address remote appearance or provide a link to where the remote instructions can be found online or were not part of the Probate division's local rules is not sufficient. Any future notices must have the printed out instructions attached to the notice. Both the mailed and published notice indicated that this was a hearing of the petition AND the amended petition. An amended pleading completely restates the supersedes the pleading it amends for all purposes. [CRC 7.3.] Notice was mailed to Ashley Johnson at an address that differs from the one reported to be hers in the amendment filed on 3/16/26. Issues raised by the Court should just be addressed by filing the missing papers (or withdrawing an erroneous filing); there is no need to declare that they will be done. The child of a first cousin is a first cousin once removed. The grandchild of a first cousin is a first cousin twice removed. The actual ages of adults do not need to be set forth in item 8 of a Petition for Probate; "Adult" is sufficient. (Ages of minors must be set forth.) Status of closing the estate will be set for 7/26/27. The administrator must file either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of a report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. C. Section 12200 (requesting additional time for estate administration) at least two weeks before 7/26/27. Appearance will be required on 7/26/27 if no petition has been filed.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF KARLA THURSTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Order To Close Conservatorship Due To The Death Of Conservatee (Final Accounting)
rulings.Rulings: Off calendar.

rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF LENORA J DONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Compliance Status Hearing
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/17/26. Attorney advises that the order will need to be amended to identify a different bank in which to deposit the funds into a blocked account. He was reminded that an ex parte petition must be filed. [LRSF 14.11 B.3.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BRIAN VANCE EVANS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/17/26. (No earlier availability.) No response to issues posted in the tentative ruling for the hearing on 4/20/26. An ex parte petition must be filed to request that notice be dispensed with to a person whose whereabouts are unknown even after diligent search. If the decedent's father's death has never been confirmed, a petition to determine fact of death should be filed. New deadline for providing courtesy copies of any responsive papers to the assigned examiner is 8/3/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE IRA KURLANDER 2024 REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST DATED MAY 8, 2024
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Assets To Trust
rulings.Rulings: Granted per written order, although notice with a copy of the first supplement was mailed before the first supplement was e-filed, and a copy of the second supplement was apparently not served. [CRC 7.53(b).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CAROLE ANNE DERRY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GARY WOOD 2004 REVOCABLE TRUST,UNDER DECLARATION OF TRUST,DATED DECEMBER 22, 2004, AS AMENDED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming That Certain Assets Are Property Of The Trust Probate Code 850 And 17200
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/24/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) A beneficiary of the trust receives mailed notice with a copy of the petition, but any heir-at-law, who would stand to inherit, if the trust did not exist or were invalid, is a party whose right, title or interest would be affected by this petition, and, therefore, must receive (or waive) personal service of notice. [Prob. C. Sect. 17203(b) and CCP Sect. 413.10 et seq.] (2) Please file a copy of the vesting deed for the real property in Rio Vista and certificates of title or vehicle registrations for the vehicles. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 8/10/26. NB: A copy of any supplement to the petition must also be served. [CRC 7.53(b).] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FREDERICK DENNIS HART
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/6/26 to address the following issues: 1. A copy of the lost will must be filed in the same way that an original will is filed. 2. A supplemental declaration, verified by the petitioner, should be filed indicating whether a full search of the residence has been completed, whether decedent had an office or safe deposit box, whether the original trust has been located, and whether any assets are currently titled in the name of the trust. All documents to be filed by 6/22/26.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/01/2026 per counsel's request (via e-mail on 05/29/2026). Notes were e-mailed to counsel on 05/29/2026.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SHUI WAH CHAN FONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/17/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service of the notice of this hearing has been filed. (3) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (4) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 8/3/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
